mission_statement: A comprehensive, queryable, and continuously updating knowledge base of the global AI industry. It tracks every significant entity — companies, people, products, models, institutions — their interconnections, pricing, benchmarks, and the daily news that moves them. Designed for automated agents to ingest and answer 'who's who', 'what costs what', 'who competes with whom', and 'what happened today in AI'.

core_philosophy.principle_1: One generic 'entities' table — no rigid type constraints. Companies, people, products, universities, VCs, and government agencies all live in the same table differentiated by entity_type. The categories table provides hierarchical organization. This avoids schema migrations when new entity types emerge.
core_philosophy.principle_2: Relationships carry the semantics, not entity types. Two entities can have any relationship type (ceo_of, competes_with, trained_on, student_of). The relationship graph is the primary query surface.
core_philosophy.principle_3: JSON attrs blobs for anything that doesn't fit fixed columns. No ALTER TABLE to add niche fields.
core_philosophy.principle_4: SQLite-first. Portable, zero-dependency, easy to clone and ship. The entire DB is one file.
core_philosophy.principle_5: Daily freshness. News-driven updates via Brave Search + HackerNews, with market snapshots from Yahoo Finance.

architecture.deployment: Hermes Agent cron job on elko homelab

data_sources.name: Brave Search API
data_sources.purpose: Primary news discovery. Searches for AI news across Reuters, MIT Tech Review, WIRED, CNBC, Bloomberg, SCMP, TechCrunch, VentureBeat, The Verge.
data_sources.how_accessed: Via Hermes Agent's MCP brave-search server (node-based, API key in config.yaml)
data_sources.queries_per_run: 3-5 (general AI news + agents/MCP + model releases + Chinese AI + hardware)

data_sources.name: HackerNews Algolia API
data_sources.purpose: Community-sourced AI stories with upvote scores for relevance ranking

data_sources.name: Yahoo Finance (via elko-market MCP)
data_sources.purpose: Stock price snapshots for AI companies

data_sources.name: SEC EDGAR (via elko-market MCP)
data_sources.purpose: Company financial data, insider trades, filings
data_sources.used_for: Financial depth on US-listed AI companies

data_sources.name: OpenRouter / AI World Graph MCP
data_sources.purpose: Model pricing tiers, benchmark scores, entity relationships
data_sources.note: These MCP servers contain overlapping data — used for cross-reference but primary source is the local DB

entities.description: Universal entity table. Every node in the knowledge graph lives here.

columns.entity_type: TEXT — no CHECK constraint. Current values: company, person, product, vc_firm, gov_agency, nonprofit, university, research_group, investment_bank, hedge_fund, blog, institution, publication, podcast
columns.name: TEXT — display name
columns.aliases: TEXT — JSON array of alternative names
columns.status: TEXT — active, acquired, merged, closed, dormant
columns.attrs: TEXT — JSON blob for any extra fields

relationships.description: Directed edges between entities. The relationship_type carries the semantics — 'ceo_of', 'competes_with', 'trained_on', 'invested_in', 'student_of', 'founded', etc.

columns.confidence: INTEGER 1-5 — how confident we are in this relationship
columns.attrs: TEXT — JSON for investment amounts, share percentages, etc.

models.description: AI/ML model cards with technical specs

events.description: Timeline of notable AI industry events

categories.description: Hierarchical entity categories (e.g., llm_providers > us_llm_providers, creative_media > video_generation)

blog_topic_suggestions.description: AI blog post ideas auto-generated from daily news

trending_tags.description: Topic frequency tracker — upserted daily from top story tags

cli_commands.list: python3 /opt/data/ai_world_helper.py list [type] — list all entities or filter by type
cli_commands.get: python3 /opt/data/ai_world_helper.py get <name> — entity details + all relationships
cli_commands.search: python3 /opt/data/ai_world_helper.py search <term> — search names, descriptions, slugs
cli_commands.model: python3 /opt/data/ai_world_helper.py model <slug> — full model card + pricing + benchmarks
cli_commands.prices: python3 /opt/data/ai_world_helper.py prices [min_params] — cheapest models by output price
cli_commands.edge: python3 /opt/data/ai_world_helper.py edge — models under 10B params for homelab

daily_pipeline.trigger: Cron job at 4AM UTC (Hermes Agent: cron job 'ai-world-daily')

steps.name: News Discovery (via Brave Search MCP)
steps.detail: Agent performs 3-5 Brave news searches with different angles. Collects 15-30 unique stories with titles, URLs, sources, snippets. Prioritizes Reuters, CNBC, Bloomberg, MIT Tech Review, WIRED, The Verge, TechCrunch, VentureBeat.

steps.name: News Fallback (via HackerNews API)
steps.detail: Script at /opt/data/ai_world_daily_update.py fetches HN Algolia API for AI-tagged stories in the last 24h. Returns sorted by points. Also provides DB stats.

steps.name: Market Data (via Yahoo Finance MCP)
steps.detail: Agent fetches current stock prices for 12 AI tickers (NVDA, AMD, TSM, ASML, AVGO, INTC, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, BABA, BIDU).

steps.name: Report Composition
steps.detail: Agent writes markdown report to /opt/data/ai_world_reports/YYYY-MM-DD.md with: top stories (with clickable links), market snapshot, DB growth stats, trending tags.

steps.name: Email Delivery
steps.detail: Query mailing DB for ai-world-daily subscribers. Send individually via SMTP (mail.elko.ai:465) with personalized greeting. Save to INBOX.Sent via IMAP. Append unsubscribe footer.

steps.name: Audit Logging
steps.detail: Log send in hermes_mailing.db sends + send_recipients tables.

current_gaps: No DEEPSEEK V4 model card or pricing yet (released Apr 24, known: V4-Flash $0.14/$0.28, V4-Pro $0.145/$3.48 per 1M tokens)
No GPT-5.5 model card or pricing yet (released Apr 23, known: $5/$30 per 1M tokens)
No Claude Opus 4.7 model card (released Apr 16)
No Claude Mythos Preview model card
Stock prices are snapshot only — no historical trend tracking
No automated entity discovery from news articles (new entities must be manually added)
No sentiment tracking per entity over time
No NER pipeline to extract entities from article text
No web UI or dashboard — CLI-only access
future_roadmap: Model refresh pipeline — add GPT-5.5, DeepSeek V4, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3.1 Pro with real pricing and benchmarks
Automated entity extraction from news (NER + entity linking to existing DB)
Weekly ecosystem sweep — research missing companies/people and bulk-insert
Stock price history tracking (daily close prices stored as time series)
Web dashboard (Streamlit or similar) for visual exploration of the graph
Graph database export (NetworkX or Neo4j) for relationship queries
MCP server mode — serve the KG as an MCP tool for other agents to query
Benchmark score tracking over time (did model X get better/worse versions?)
Cloud deployment (Modal or similar) for public API access
